extraction.one_line_summary: HODEG Danny won after MARKOV Michael went early at 300m, beating ACKERMANN Pascal who gambled on inside traffic and got boxed in.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step neutralized Lotto-Jumbo numerical advantage by launching Markov leadout early at 300m, catching Ackermann too far back.
Ackermann repeatedly choosing inside line gambles backfired when traffic appeared, suggesting positioning discipline beats pure speed.
Markov's Danish Champion-level strength allowed him to lead out from 300m and still nearly win himself, unusual durability.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: After two stage wins, positional indiscipline cost him - chose inside traffic gamble and was 6-8 lengths back when Markov attacked
rider_signals.MARKOV Michael: positive: Danish champion showed unusual diesel power, leading from 300m and nearly holding second himself despite full leadout effort
rider_signals.HODEG Danny: positive: Finally broke Ackermann's winning sequence by recognizing Markov's early jump timing was optimal to avoid traffic chaos
rider_signals.SABATINI Fabio: neutral: Quick-Step anchor moved early as intended but Markov's surprise timing mattered more than positioning setup
